title: 'If we catch them... ': Trump threatens China with 50% tariff if it helps Iran militarily
content: President Trump has issued a stern warning to China, threatening a "staggering" 50% tariff if Beijing aids Iran militarily. He expressed confidence Iran would yield to US demands, asserting that tough rhetoric brought them to the negotiating table. Trump also detailed plans to blockade the Strait of Hormuz and warned of severe retaliation against any Iranian aggression.

title: Strait to crisis: Trump orders Hormuz blockade after talks sink
content: Trump has declared an immediate naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz following failed diplomatic talks with Iran. Trump stated Iran's alleged "extortion" would cease, vowing military action if necessary. This move, potentially illegal under international law, threatens global energy supplies, impacting major Asian economies and allies, while potentially benefiting the US.

title: Microsoft VP says even if companies lays off 50% workforce due to AI, business will increase
content: Microsoft's Rajesh Jha counters fears of an AI-driven software slump, arguing that AI agents will require their own licenses.  He posits that as companies deploy thousands of these digital assistants, the number of 'paying users' could actually surge, even as human workforces shrink. This perspective challenges the notion that AI will diminish software revenue by reducing human users.

title: Rohit Sharma retires hurt; Ritika Sajdeh's reaction breaks the internet
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru posted a formidable 240/4, powered by Phil Salt's 78 and Rajat Patidar's rapid 53. Virat Kohli contributed a steady 50. Mumbai Indians' chase faltered early as Rohit Sharma retired hurt on 19 due to a hamstring injury, leaving the daunting target unmet.

title: Record alert! Virat Kohli eclipses Babar Azam
content: Virat Kohli and Phil Salt's explosive 120-run opening partnership powered Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a strong start against Mumbai Indians. Salt's rapid 78 set the tone, while Kohli's 50 marked his 47th T20 century partnership, the most in the format. This stand laid a crucial foundation for RCB's innings.

title: Meta's most-popular former employee and father of AI calls Anthropic's latest model as 'drama'
content: Yann LeCun, Meta's former chief AI scientist and deep learning pioneer, has dismissed the panic around Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview as "BS from self-delusion." While the model's reported ability to find vulnerabilities in every major operating system and browser spooked Wall Street and triggered an emergency Fed meeting, LeCun and other AI researchers argue the threat is overhyped—though cybersecurity firms using Mythos disagree.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office Day 25: Ranveer's film is set to cross Rs 1080 crore in India
content: In a groundbreaking move for Bollywood, Ranveer Singh's action-packed thriller 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has taken the box office by storm, eclipsing the Rs 1000 crore net milestone in India. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the film is maintaining its momentum in the fourth week of release, attracting enthusiastic audiences and raking in impressive collections both daily and weekly. Read on to know more in detail.

title: China mapping ocean around India to avert its own Hormuz moment in Strait of Malacca?
content: At the heart of this push lies a strategic concern long associated with Beijing: securing critical sea lanes, particularly through the Strait of Malacca, and reducing vulnerabilities in the event of a conflict that could threaten its energy and trade lifelines. This vulnerability has a direct bearing on India’s strategic calculus as well. Located astride key Indian Ocean routes and in proximity to the Malacca chokepoint.

title: Quote of the day: Desire beyond this world points to another, says CS Lewis
content: C.S. Lewis, a renowned 20th-century writer, shaped literature and theology. Born in Ireland, his life journey included loss, intellectual growth, and a profound return to Christianity. His clear, engaging style made complex ideas accessible. Lewis authored influential Christian apologetics and the beloved fantasy series 'The Chronicles of Narnia'. His impactful radio talks and personal life also left a lasting mark.

title: France says no to Microsoft Windows and the reason is America
content: France is ditching Microsoft Windows for Linux, a significant step in Europe's push for digital sovereignty and reduced reliance on American tech giants. This move, aimed at regaining control over data and digital infrastructure, aligns with broader EU efforts to lessen dependence on foreign technology. The initiative begins with government digital agencies, signaling a strategic shift in national digital policy.

title: Melanie Collins drops beach snap, gives glimpse of relaxing offseason
content: Melanie Collins closed her eighth NFL season with CBS after covering the NFC Championship Game, continuing her steady rise as a trusted sideline reporter. Working alongside Kevin Harlan and Trent Green, she has become a consistent presence in high-profile broadcasts. With a background that includes ESPN, Yahoo Sports, and major global events, Collins brings depth and versatility to her role. As the offseason begins, she is taking time away before an expected return for the next NFL season.

title: Andreescu’s serve error hands Putintseva win, dents Canada hopes
content: Bianca Andreescu’s missed serve on match point helped Kazakhstan defeat Canada 3-1 in the Billie Jean King Cup Qualifiers in Astana. Yulia Putintseva sealed the win in a tight three-set match. Kazakhstan advanced to the 2026 Finals, while Canada will now play in the Playoffs as Andreescu continues her comeback season.

title: Stephen Curry, Ayesha Curry make noble move, continue giving back
content: Stephen Curry, the NBA legend, sparked shockwaves when he ended his decade long partnership with Under Armour in 2025, after over 10 years of being with the brand exclusively. While Stephen Curry remained vague about the reason behind it, it seems there was a pretty noble thought that led to this decision and his wife, Ayesha Curry, has been a supporter through all of it.

title: TMC supports 'tukde-tukde gang' to cut Siliguri Corridor: PM Modi in Bengal rally
content: Prime Minister Modi alleged a 'tukde-tukde gang' threatened to sever the Siliguri Corridor, accusing the TMC of supporting them. He criticized the TMC's appeasement politics, claiming it caused suffering and stalled development, including vital infrastructure projects. Modi promised a special package for North Bengal and urged voters to support the BJP for security and jobs.

title: ‘Urdu-speaking’ assembly seats in UP record lower voter deletions
content: Urdu-speaking Assembly constituencies in Uttar Pradesh, many with significant Muslim populations, have shown lower voter deletion rates and slightly higher addition rates compared to the state average. These 46 constituencies saw 26.33 lakh deletions and added 11.79 lakh voters during the recent revision period.

title: Viral video of man harassing woman on Jaipur road sparks outrage; Police spring into action
content: A viral video from Jaipur showing two men allegedly harassing a woman on a motorcycle has sparked outrage. Rajasthan Police swiftly identified the accused using technology and informants, emphasizing that no one is above the law. Authorities also warned against endangering public safety for social media fame.

title: How Iran-US mediation in Pak collapsed amid questions over China’s silent influence
content: Iran-US negotiations in Islamabad concluded without a breakthrough, revealing deep strategic divides and jeopardizing a fragile ceasefire. Despite Pakistan's mediation, disagreements over Iran's nuclear program and control of the Strait of Hormuz led to a stalemate, with both sides blaming each other for the failure.

title: Proxies and levers: The Middle East conundrum
content: The Middle East's conflicts are analyzed through the lens of 'proxies and levers,' where state actors use non-state groups to project power and achieve strategic goals. This approach, while avoiding direct military costs, often results in prolonged violence, instability, and humanitarian crises, complicating peace efforts in the region.

title: 'Baseless narratives': India says China renaming places in Arunachal 'cannot alter reality'
content: India has strongly refuted China's latest attempt to rename places in Arunachal Pradesh. New Delhi described the move as a mischievous effort to assert false claims. Such actions by China cannot alter the reality that Arunachal Pradesh is an integral part of India. These attempts undermine efforts to stabilize bilateral ties between the two nations.

title: Virat Kohli becomes first batter to pull off this rare feat vs MI
content: Virat Kohli's explosive entry at Wankhede Stadium saw him become the first batter to score over 1,000 runs against Mumbai Indians in IPL history. He partnered with Phil Salt for a dominant 120-run opening stand, with Salt contributing a rapid 78. Mumbai Indians opted to field after winning the toss.

title: Shubman Gill creates history, breaks Virat Kohli's record to become ...
content: Shubman Gill's fluent half-century and Jos Buttler's explosive finishing guided Gujarat Titans to a seven-wicket victory over Lucknow Super Giants. Gill also achieved a significant milestone, becoming the youngest player to reach 4,000 IPL runs. The win propels GT to fifth in the standings.

title: 'Tactical drills & special skills': All about India, Uzbekistan joint exercise 'DUSTLIK'
content: An Indian Army contingent has departed for the 7th edition of the India-Uzbekistan joint military exercise, "DUSTLIK," to be held in Uzbekistan. The exercise, focusing on joint operations in semi-mountainous terrain, will enhance military cooperation and interoperability. It aims to test tactical drills and special operations to neutralize unlawful armed groups.

title: 'I'm shy of my achievements ...': When Asha Bhosle reflected on her glorious legacy
content: Legendary singer Asha Bhosle passed away at 92, leaving behind a profound musical legacy.  Known for her prolific career and adaptability, she remained a constant through every era of Indian music.  Her words, "I am not done yet," now resonate deeply, reflecting a life dedicated to artistic exploration and a humble spirit despite immense global acclaim.

title: Why RCB, MI players are wearing black armbands - Explained
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Mumbai Indians players wore black armbands to honor the legendary Asha Bhosle, who passed away at 92. Before the highly anticipated clash, a moment of silence was observed. Both teams sought to recover from recent defeats, with Virat Kohli holding a historical edge over Rohit Sharma in their IPL encounters.

title: Gill, Buttler fifties and Prasidh’s four-for power GT to commanding win over LSG
content: Gujarat Titans clinched a commanding seven-wicket victory over Lucknow Super Giants, powered by Shubman Gill and Jos Buttler's half-centuries. Prasidh Krishna's stellar 4/24 performance restricted LSG to 164/8. Gill and Buttler's dominant 85-run partnership laid the foundation for a comfortable chase, ensuring GT reached the target with eight balls to spare.

title: Baisakhi 2026: Top 50 wishes, messages and quotes to share with your loved ones
content: Celebrate Baisakhi 2026 with heartfelt wishes and messages. This article offers 60 diverse options, from simple greetings to reflective quotes, perfect for sharing with loved ones. It emphasizes the festival's significance for the Sikh community and its broader appeal for fresh starts and genuine happiness.

title: ‘Kicked in abdomen repeatedly’: UP man booked for killing unborn children over dowry demands
content: In Bhadohi, a woman has accused her husband and two in-laws of causing the death of her unborn children through dowry-related assaults during pregnancies. The complaint details repeated physical abuse, including kicks to the abdomen, leading to two miscarriages. Authorities have registered a case under relevant sections of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and the Dowry Prohibition Act.

title: 'She was family': Sachin Tendulkar mourns Asha Bhosle's passing away
content: Legendary singer Asha Bhosle passed away at 92 in Mumbai on Sunday morning after being admitted to Breach Candy Hospital with cardiac and respiratory complications. Her last public appearance was at Arjun Tendulkar's wedding on March 5, 2026. Sachin Tendulkar expressed his grief, calling her 'family' and highlighting her timeless musical legacy.

title: Vance says Trump called ‘a dozen times’ during peace talks with Iran
content: US Vice President JD Vance revealed President Trump was in constant contact during Pakistan peace talks with Iran. Negotiations stalled over Iran's nuclear program, with the US demanding an end to enrichment. Iran deemed US demands unreasonable, citing past distrust, while Pakistan pledged to facilitate future dialogue.

title: From cabaret to classical: The many lives of Asha Bhosale, India’s timeless voice
content: Legendary Indian singer Asha Bhosle, a global icon with a 70-year career, has passed away at 92. Known for her versatile voice and numerous hit songs, she was recognized by Guinness World Records as the most recorded artist. Bhosle also ventured into composing and acting, leaving an indelible mark on music and cinema.

title: 'Ready to mediate': Putin tells Iran president after peace talks with US fails
content: Russian President Vladimir Putin offered to mediate peace efforts in the Middle East during a call with Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian. This offer follows the collapse of US-Iran talks on a nuclear agreement, raising concerns about a fragile ceasefire. Russia also criticized US actions regarding Iran, calling them a pretext for regime change

title: Cash-strapped Pakistan to receive $5bn from Saudi, Qatar as debt mounts
content: Pakistan is set to receive $5 billion in financial support from Saudi Arabia and Qatar, easing pressure on its external position and foreign exchange reserves. This crucial aid comes as Islamabad prepares to repay $3.5 billion to the UAE this month, with Finance Minister Aurangzeb engaging in economic outreach at the IMF-World Bank Spring Meetings.

title: Kerala woman ‘sexually harassed’ on way to hospital after accident
content: A woman injured in an accident in Kayamkulam has filed a complaint alleging sexual harassment by a man who helped shift her to the hospital. The accused was questioned and released, prompting the victim to claim lapses in the investigation. State police chief has sought a detailed report and an internal probe is underway.

title: How Rory McIlroy's Saturday stumble sets up a dramatic Sunday at the Masters
content: After losing the biggest 36-hole lead in Masters history, McIlroy and Augusta will have another spin Sunday.

title: Sparks acquire All-Star Atkins in trade with Sky
content: The Sky have traded two-time All-Star and Olympic gold medalist Ariel Atkins to the Sparks for 2024 No. 4 overall pick Rickea Jackson.

title: Frustrated Garcia breaks driver on No. 2 tee box
content: Sergio García and Jon Rahm were in the same group for the final round of the Masters, a Spanish duo of former champions that added some buzz to the early pairings of players out of contention.

title: Three-time All-Star, former manager Garner dies
content: Phil Garner, a three-time All-Star infielder who went on to manage the Astros to their first World Series appearance, has died. He was 76.

title: Smesko says Reese will 'fit in great' with Dream
content: Atlanta Dream coach Karl Smesko said Sunday that newly acquired forward Angel Reese will "fit in great," but added the team will work to improve her shooting efficiency.

title: Sinner outduels Alcaraz to retake No. 1 ranking
content: Jannik Sinner reclaimed the world No. 1 ranking by overpowering Carlos Alcaraz to win the Monte-Carlo Masters final.

title: Avs coach suffers facial fractures, out 'til playoffs
content: Coach Jared Bednar will not accompany the Avalanche on their road trip to western Canada to end the regular season after taking a puck to the face.

title: Ex-Ohio St. WR Ginn arrested, misses UFL game
content: Former NFL wide receiver and current Aviators coach Ted Ginn Jr. was charged with driving while intoxicated in Tarrant County, Texas, on Saturday.

title: 🏈 NFL draft: Seven prospects climbing boards
content: Dillon Thieneman and Monroe Freeling have drastically boosted their stock since the start of last season. Who else has climbed the board?

title: NBA playoff watch: What's at stake on Sunday
content: We break down every seeding possibility, plus a look at the race to secure the best odds for the No. 1 pick.

title: Moving day: Young ties Rory amid Masters drama
content: Cameron Young, who started the day eight back of Rory McIlroy, shot a 7-under 65 and is tied with the defending champion entering the final round of the Masters.

title: WNBA free agency and trade tracker: Plum back to Sparks, McBride back to Lynx
content: WNBA free agency is here. Kelsey Plum is returning to Los Angeles. Kayla McBride is returning to Minnesota.

title: How Keyshawn Johnson made draft history 30 years ago with the Jets
content: The 1996 draft didn't produce a first-round QB, but the Jets found a playmaker with star power in Johnson.

title: Tottenham's relegation looks increasingly inevitable after loss at Sunderland
content: Now that Spurs have dropped into the relegation zone, there is a dawning realization that one of the Premier League's Big Six might actually go down.

title: Who should challenge for Carlos Ulberg's championship first?
content: Carlos Ulberg won the UFC light heavyweight championship, but an obstacle may prevent him from defending it anytime soon.

title: Fury, Joshua and Wilder are back, what's next for the glamour division?
content: Tyson Fury won and called out Anthony Joshua, and with Deontay Wilder back, the heavyweight division is buzzing.

title: UCLA and its six seniors are eyeing a WNBA draft takeover
content: Can UCLA make history and have all six seniors -- who scored all of the team's points at the Final Four -- drafted in the WNBA?

content: Will Lauren Betts, Awa Fam, Azzi Fudd or Olivia Miles go No. 1, and which transfers boosted their draft stock?

content: NBA players are spending more time than ever working on the boards -- but we're not talking rebounds.

content: Dive into how women's professional wrestling has evolved on WWE's biggest stage over the past 40 years.

content: Seattle's biggest stars have struggled at the plate as the team has gotten off to rough start this season. How real are the concerns? Let's find out.

content: Teams with championship hopes fall down our list while unexpected contenders have made surprise starts.

title: Rankings: Top 100 players for 2026
content: Plan for the 2026 Fantasy Women's Basketball season with the top 100 rankings, including player position ranks.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal, Man City must act fast for Sandro Tonali
content: Newcastle have issued their terms for a Sandro Tonali move, with Arsenal and Man City showing interest. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Trinamool Appeasing "Tukde Tukde Gang": In Bengal, PM Invokes National Security
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi said the Trinamool had rewarded a "Tukde-tukde gang" which threatened to snap the northeat away from India.

title: PM Modi To Address Special Conference In Delhi Ahead Of Women's Quota Bill
content: The conference - which PM Modi will address -- is being organised by the Ministry of Women and Child Development under the slogan: "From Panchayat to Parliament-Women in Decision-Making: Preparing for a New India".

title: Trump Watched Fist Fights At UFC As US, Iran Wrestled At Peace Talks
content: Trump entered the Kaseya Centre shortly after 9 pm to watch the light heavyweight fight between Jiri Prochazka and Carlos Ulberg.

title: While US-Iran Talked Peace In Pak, A Netanyahu Phone Call Changed Everything
content: The collapse of the US-Iran talks comes at a fragile moment as a two-week ceasefire brokered earlier this month is now under renewed strain with just nine days left

title: Global Common India Has To Manage: Ram Madhav At Indian Ocean Conference
content: "What unfortunately happens mostly is there are certain big powers in the world which want to play the role of managers of all the global commons," said Ram Madhav, who is also the chief of Delhi-based think tank India Foundation, one of the organisers of the conference.

title: Trump's "Final Offer" Pitch: Seen In Ukraine, China, North Korea...Now Iran
content: On Sunday, after 21 hours of talks in Islamabad produced no agreement with Iran, US Vice-President JD Vance invoked the phrase on Donald Trump's behalf.

title: US-Israel-Iran War LIVE Updates: Trump Says US To Block Hormuz, Threatens China With 50% Tariffs
content: Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf has arrived in Islamabad for peace talks with US, JD Vance is said to be en route.

title: 2 Reasons Why US-Iran Talks Failed, According To Donald Trump
content: Donald Trump claimed the Iranians want money and nuclear weapons, but the US armed forces are "locked and loaded" to finish "the little that is left of Iran".

title: Probe Agency ED In Overdrive In Bengal, Launches Action In Flurry Of Cases
content: Throughout late March and early April 2026, the federal agency has targeted political heavyweights, senior officials, and corporate entities, uncovering hawala transactions.

title: Arrest At Sub-Zero Temperature: Delhi Cops' Massive Feat Near China Border
content: The Delhi Police has been running Operation CyHawk to bust cyber fraud rackets. In February, they arrested 955 people.

title: 'Give $1 Billion, Most Beautiful Woman As Wife': Uganda Army Chief To Turkey
content: He has also threatened to cut diplomatic ties with Turkey and close its embassy in Kampala within 30 days if his demands are not met.

title: Netaji's Grandnephew Joins Trinamool Ahead of Bengal Polls
content: Chandra Bose, grandnephew of Subhas Chandra Bose and a former BJP leader, joined the Trinamool Congress on Sunday, days ahead of the West Bengal assembly elections.

title: Putin Offers To Help Middle East Peace Efforts In Call With Iran President
content: "Vladimir Putin emphasised his readiness to further facilitate the search for a political and diplomatic settlement to the conflict," the Kremlin said in its readout of the call.

title: 163 Minor Boys Being Trafficked To Maharashtra Rescued From Train In Madhya Pradesh: Cops
content: As many as 163 minor boys, allegedly being trafficked from Bihar to Maharashtra to work as labourers, were rescued from an express train in Madhya Pradesh's Katni district, the railway police said on Sunday.

title: Video: Speeding Omni Overturns, Hits Women And Children On Karnataka Road
content: A woman was seriously injured after a speeding Omni van lost control and ran into pedestrians at Kolpada Bailu in Beltangady taluk of Karnataka's Mangaluru on Sunday afternoon.

title: US And Iran's Claims On American Warships Sail Into Strait Of 'Confusion'
content: Iran rubbished the CENTCOM version, with the Islamic nation's military spokesperson saying "the initiative for the passage and movement of any vessel is in the hands of the armed forces of the Islamic Republic of Iran"

title: "Can't Alter Undeniable Reality": India On China Giving Fake Names To Areas
content: Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal in a statement said India "categorically rejects any mischievous attempts by the Chinese side to assign fictitious names to places which form part of the territory of India"

content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: Withdraw amendments made to Private Universities Act: SFI
content: Recent amendments facilitate setting up more private universities, promoting commercialisation of education, say SFI leaders

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Cash-for-votes storm dims Sobha Surendran’s sheen
content: Jamaat-e-Islami serves legal notice on Sobha Surendran for blaming it for the murder of RSS leader Sreenivasan in Palakkad on April 16, 2022

title: Asha Bhosle: the voice that defied genres, time
content: Asha’s voice felt like a friend, a co-conspirator, or a mirror — someone who had been through the messiness of life and still chose to sing, dance, and seduce with full-throated joy; the most-recorded artist will be remembered for her fearless spirit, versatility, and playful charm

title: If Centre stops assistance or grants, we will not accept it, says K.P. Munusamy
content: It’s true that we backed the farm laws that had benefits for farmers, but it was Edappadi K. Palaniswami who stalled the project for the extraction of mineral resources in the delta, says the AIADMK deputy general secretary

title: Vijayawada-born social entrepreneur to receive global honour
content: Founder and CEO of Indus Action Tarun Cherukuri to receive award at Skoll World Forum, scheduled to be held in Oxford, U.K. on April 23

content: The External Affairs Ministry said China should refrain from actions which inject negativity into relations and undermine efforts to create better understanding

content: Tamil Nadu Social Welfare Minister P. Geetha Jeevan says number of cases registered, chargesheets filed, and convictions secured have increased; highlighting steps taken to ensure fresh eggs for noon day meals, she promises efforts to ease traffic woes, boost healthcare and create more jobs in Thoothukudi

content: Emphasising the strategic importance of Siliguri Corridor, the Prime Minister says Trinamool supports “tukde-tukde” gang that had threatened to snap the corridor to cut off the Northeast from the rest of the country

title: BJP issues whip for MPs ahead of special Parliament session
content: In a message to all of its MPs, the BJP said, "A Three-Line Whip being issued to all BJP Members in the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha from Thursday to Saturday, 16th to 18th April 2026."

title: Cashew hub to witness high-stakes battle between AIADMK and VCK
content: K. Mohan of the AIADMK will be taking on Abdul Rahman of the VCK in Panruti; there are nine candidates in the fray

content: “Conveyed warm greetings of Prime Minister @narendramodi and our gratitude for ensuring well-being of the Indian community during the West Asia conflict,” says S. Jaishankar

content: It has been informed that approval for utilisation is governed by guidelines issued by the Ministry of Home Affairs

title: Asha Bhosle dies at 92; PM Modi, Raj Thackeray lead tributes
content: To be cremated with full State honours; last rites will be performed at Shivaji Park in Mumbai at 4 p.m.

title: Rijiju writes to Kharge, says delay in women’s quota law will deny justice to millions
content: This is not about politics. This is about keeping our promise to the daughters of India. Let us rise above hesitation & move forward, together, for 'Nari Shakti’, says Union Minister Kiren Rijiju in a letter to Congress President Mallikarjun Kharge

title: ED steps up action in multiple West Bengal cases amid elections
content: Cases include the primary teachers’ recruitment scam, alleged diversion of PDS wheat meant for welfare schemes, ‘illegal’ coal mining, money laundering, the alleged municipal recruitment scam, land grabbing, and siphoning of funds

title: Shivraj Singh Chouhan to oversee election of BJP legislature party leader in Bihar
content: Chief Minister Nitish Kumar calls Cabinet meeting on April 14, may resign after formally informing Cabinet colleagues about his exit

title: BJP plans to divide Bengal using the delimitation process: Mamata
content: TMC supremo flags the BJP’s plan to ‘trifurcate’ Bengal; links voter deletions to NRC push and calls for voter vigilance with regard to EVMs

title: 12 Indian fishermen from Tamil Nadu, Karaikal arrested by Sri Lankan Navy
content: While they were fishing about 60 nautical miles off Karaikal, the boat was intercepted by the Sri Lankan Navy

title: Kannur dental college student death raises questions about casteism in campuses
content: Parents alleged that certain faculty members discriminated against Nithin Raj on caste lines and showed a skin tone bias; CPI(M), Congress, and student organisations cutting across political lines have condemned the incident and demanded a comprehensive enquiry

title: Amaravati to host India’s first quantum test facilities on April 14
content: Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu to launch 1S and 1Q test beds under Quantum Valley project, positioning Andhra Pradesh as a hub for next-generation computing and research

title: Kerala Assembly polls: High polling figures redraw political calculations in Pathanamthitta
content: Political leaders attribute the spike in polling to the charged campaign atmosphere powered by high-voltage rallies led by the PM, CM and Rahul Gandhi

title: Software engineer from Vizianagaram drowns in U.S. river on his birthday
content: Singireddy Sai Krishna, 29, a software engineer from Bobbili in Vizianagaram district, drowns in the Meramec river in Missouri while celebrating his birthday

title: Traditional community diggers create percolation wells in Bengaluru to augment groundwater
content: By channelling rainwater directly into underground aquifers, they replenish the natural water bank, eventually raising the water table enough to refill dry wells, says Vishwanath S., director of Biome Environmental Solutions

title: Press Council of India issues election reporting advisory to media
content: The PCI has advised print media to follow the Guidelines on Election Reporting and Norms of Journalistic Conduct-2022

title: State Finance Commission recommends probe into power billing of panchayat installations
content: It expressed concern over the huge discrepancies in billing and lack of coordination among authorities that has resulted in accruing of interest amount running into thousands of crores

title: Lorry owners urge Centre to set up Transport Ombudsman
content: A. P. association flags e-Challan errors, tax validity mismatch and rising toll costs; seeks uniform diesel pricing, fair enforcement and relief on fitness fees

title: Disruptions in cooking gas, diesel supply continue to affect fisheries sector
content: Around 2,000 engaged in fishing-related work at Thoppumpady harbour hit as deep-sea vessels, including gillnet, longline, and trawl boats, remain idle

title: New fire station planned in Rayapudi to combat frequent accidents in Amaravati
content: The accidents were primarily caused by prolonged stocking of conduit pipes and other materials, poor maintenance, lack of water supply, and absence of firefighting equipment at the yards, says Fire Services in-charge Director General

title: Sajjala accuses A.P. CM of presenting inflated figures on Amaravati project
content: Naidu’s claim that Amaravati is a self-financing project backed by a 5,000-acre land bank worth over ₹50,000 crore is far from reality, says the YSRCP leader

title: Women's transfer rankings for 2026-27 season
content: the women's college basketball transfer portal opened Monday with a tidal wave of activity . more than 1,300 players are looking for new homes . the portal closes April 20 but players' decisions for their next school can still come after that .

title: UFC 327 takeaways: Ulberg blew out his knee, but Procházka blew his title chance
content: Carlos Ulberg grabbed the light heavyweight championship out of the jaws of defeat at UFC 327 in Miami on sunday . the heavyweights on the main card, Josh Hokit and Curtis Blaydes, put on a slugfest for the ages . a former Bellator MMA star who had a horrible debut proved first impressions aren't everything .

content: the final few weeks of the regular season feature the best teams preparing for the playoffs, the worst teams tanking, banged-up star players being shut down and plenty of outlier situations unfolding . coaches, scouts and executives decide whether what they're seeing on the court is real or not .

title: Sources: Kings to retain Christie for next season
content: despite a 22-59 record, the Kings are retaining coach Doug Christie for next season . the Kings initially stepped into the job in December 2024 . new general manager Scott Perry signed him to a three-year deal .

title: NHL playoff watch: The clinching scenarios for Sunday
content: out west, the Vegas Golden Knights clinched their spot with an overtime win over the Colorado Avalanche . the St. Louis Blues and Seattle Kraken were knocked out . there is one spot left in the East (the no. 3 seed in the Metro) and two spots open in the West .

title: News: George Springer fractures toe; O's Adley Rutschman to IL
content: fantasy baseball buzz file aims to provide fantasy managers with the intel they need as news breaks around MLB . the final game of the fantasy week features the Atlanta Braves hosting the Cleveland Guardians at 7:20 p.m. ET . Jeremy Pena was lifted from the contest with a sore right knee .

title: Roman Reigns' Top 10 WrestleMania main events: Which match finished No. 1?
content: Roman Reigns will challenge CM Punk at WrestleMania 42 Night 2 on April 18 at Allegiant Stadium in Las Vegas for the world heavyweight championship in his 11th main event . he defeated Brock Lesnar at the "Winner Take All" main event between lesnar and thera .

title: Men's transfer rankings: The top 60 players in the portal right now
content: more than 1,000 men's basketball players added their names to the transfer portal . the portal drew around 2,100 players in 2024 and nearly 2,700 players last year . most coaches expect this season's portal entrants to surpass the 3,000 mark .
